completeness.rationale: The triage captured most expected information: 2-4 hour delay, started ~1 week ago, worse at 9-10am/better in afternoon, connection to the daily digest feature, queue congestion/competition hypothesis, and the 1-2 minute baseline. The one gap is not explicitly identifying that transactional and bulk emails are competing for the same pipeline (the priority queue mechanism), though the summary does gesture at 'competing with real-time notification delivery'.

accuracy.rationale: The triage correctly identified the digest feature as the cause and queue congestion as the mechanism, which matches an acceptable diagnostic path. The hypothesis that 'background processing is competing with real-time notification delivery' is directionally correct. It doesn't identify the specific priority inversion mechanism (bulk emails sharing priority=1 with transactional emails), but the acceptable paths explicitly allow identifying digest/queue congestion without understanding the priority system. No significant inaccuracies.

thoroughness.rationale: The agent resolved after just one round of Q&A. The reporter's mention of the digest feature was a strong signal that warranted at least one targeted follow-up — e.g., asking whether the timing of the digest send correlates with the 9am delay spike, or whether IT had checked the email queue backlog. The information_gaps section acknowledges these missing pieces, but they should have been asked before resolving.

economy.rationale: The first question bundled four sub-questions efficiently. The email provider question turned out to be low-value (the issue is client/provider-independent), but it was reasonable to ask before ruling it out. Overall the turn usage was efficient given the quick resolution.

question_quality.rationale: The initial question was well-structured and hit the key diagnostic dimensions (version, hosting model, email provider, recent changes). The 'has anything changed recently?' prompt was well-placed and directly elicited the digest feature mention. The question about email provider was less valuable but not harmful. The agent didn't ask a follow-up to dig into the 9am timing correlation after the digest feature was mentioned, which would have been the most insightful possible question.

actionability.rationale: The recommended_fix section gives a developer clear, specific investigation paths: check queue/scheduler modifications, check routing of real-time notifications through the digest pipeline, check if digest background job is starving the notification worker. A developer could immediately go look at email sending code changes from the digest feature rollout. The proposed test case is concrete. Minor gap: not pointing to the priority queue configuration specifically.

notable_strengths: Efficiently identified the digest feature connection from a reporter hint and built a coherent, actionable hypothesis around it
Correctly characterized the time-of-day pattern (9am worse, afternoon better) in the root cause hypothesis, linking it to queue drain behavior
Recommended fix is specific and gives a developer multiple concrete threads to pull on
Acknowledged information gaps honestly, including the key validation step (disable digest feature to confirm hypothesis)
notable_weaknesses: Resolved after a single exchange without following up on the digest feature mention — a question about whether the digest sends at 9am would have strongly confirmed the hypothesis
Did not ask about server-side queue monitoring or logs before resolving, which would have quickly confirmed or refuted the congestion theory
Email provider question (Gmail/Outlook/Exchange) added noise since the issue was ultimately server-side
most_insightful_question: The bundled question asking 'Has anything changed in your infrastructure or configuration around the time this started?' — this open-ended prompt directly elicited the reporter's mention of the digest feature rollout, which was the key diagnostic clue.
missed_opportunities: Ask whether the marketing digest emails are sent at a specific time each morning (e.g., 9am), which would directly confirm the timing correlation
Ask whether IT could check the email queue depth or backlog around 9am to quantify the congestion
Ask whether temporarily disabling the digest feature had been tried or could be tried as a quick diagnostic step
